title: CandleRuntime adapter scaffolding (candle-core + candle-transformers)
goal: Create the CandleRuntime adapter module: public adapter struct, candle-core + candle-transformers dependency wiring, ModelRuntime impl skeleton, Device selection (CPU/CUDA/Metal), tokenizer integration via tokenizers crate. Subsequent MTs fill in real implementations (activation hooks, SSMs).
expected_diff_shape: New directory src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/candle/ with mod.rs, adapter.rs (CandleRuntime struct + skeleton ModelRuntime impl), device.rs (Device selector), tokenizer.rs (HF tokenizers wrapper); registry update.
unblock_conditions: MT-061..MT-070 trait + sub-traits + registry + LlmClient routing merged.
implementation_notes: Per AC-CANDLE-ADAPTER (spec §4.6.2): CandleRuntime wraps candle-core + candle-transformers; required path for hook-requiring techniques (activation steering, refusal vector, CAA) and for subquadratic architectures (Mamba2, RWKV v5-v7). CandleRuntime struct: `pub struct CandleRuntime { models: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<ModelId, CandleModelHandle>>>, device: candle_core::Device, tokenizer_cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<ModelId, Arc<tokenizers::Tokenizer>>>>, ledger: Arc<ProcessLedgerIntegration> }`. CandleModelHandle is an enum: `Transformer { model: Box<dyn TransformerModel>, config: TransformerConfig, state: TransformerState }`, `Mamba2 { model: candle_transformers::models::mamba2::Model, state: Mamba2State }`, `RwkvV5 { model: ..., state: ... }`, `RwkvV6 { ... }`, `RwkvV7 { ... }`. (Mamba2/RWKV variants implemented in MT-085/MT-086/MT-087.) candle_core::Device selection per OS+GPU: try Cuda(0) if cuda feature enabled and available; fallback Metal(0) on macOS; fallback Cpu. Operator override via LoadSpec.device_override. Tokenizer cache: HF tokenizers crate loads `tokenizer.json` from the model directory; embedded in CandleModelHandle for fast access. impl ModelRuntime for CandleRuntime: load verifies RuntimeKind==Candle, sha256, mints ModelId v7, registers with ledger; generate/score/embed return NotImplemented (filled MT-082..MT-088); kv_cache returns Err CapabilityNotSupported for Mamba2/RWKV variants (they use state-vector cache, MT-088 separate handle), Default impl for Transformer variant; steering_hooks returns SteeringHookHandle backed by CandleSteeringHook (MT-082). All candle_core::* / candle_transformers::* identifiers MUST stay inside model_runtime/candle/**.
risk_if_missed: CandleRuntime cannot be wired into LlmClient; hook-requiring techniques and SSMs have no host.
validator_focus: Grep candle_core:: / candle_transformers:: only inside model_runtime/candle/**; Device selector respects operator override; ModelId v7.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Device selector cuda-not-available path returns CPU fallback (no panic).
Cargo feature flags for cuda/metal documented so non-GPU builds compile.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Scaffold + ModelRuntime impl skeleton lands cleanly with engine-containment, sha256 verification, UUID v7, OS device selector, tokenizer cache, and feature-gated native imports. Concerns: (a) `load_duration_ms` is measured BEFORE any safetensors construction work (mmap, model build, state init), so the metric is meaningless / monotonically near-zero; (b) `CandleModelBackend` is enum with engine-feature variants only and emits a non-helpful `not_implemented("candle_*")` for SSM paths via the catch-all `_` arm in `generate`, but that arm is now unreachable for the engine-on build (good) — yet the `kv_cache` fallback for Transformer returns `candle_kv_cache not implemented` even though MT-088 should later wire a Transformer KV handle; (c) `device_kind` reports `Cuda { ordinal: 0 }` / `Metal { ordinal: 0 }` regardless of the actual ordinal returned by candle, so a non-zero ordinal device collapses to 0 in the selection record (telemetry / receipts will be wrong).
hbr_unsatisfied: HBR-INT-002: required wire test proves ContextBundle -> ModelAdapter -> ToolGate -> ArtifactStore chain; no such evidence in MT-081 tests, and CandleRuntime does not appear to wire any ledger/ArtifactStore integration in adapter.rs (the narrative's `ledger: Arc<ProcessLedgerIntegration>` is not in the struct).

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Scaffold + ModelRuntime impl skeleton lands cleanly with engine-containment, sha256 verification, UUID v7, OS device selector, tokenizer cache, and feature-gated native imports. Concerns: (a) `load_duration_ms` is measured BEFORE any safetensors construction work (mmap, model build, state init), so the metric is meaningless / monotonically near-zero; (b) `CandleModelBackend` is enum with engine-feature variants only and emits a non-helpful `not_implemented("candle_*")` for SSM paths via the catch-all `_` arm in `generate`, but that arm is now unreachable for the engine-on build (good) — yet the `kv_cache` fallback for Transformer returns `candle_kv_cache not implemented` even though MT-088 should later wire a Transformer KV handle; (c) `device_kind` reports `Cuda { ordinal: 0 }` / `Metal { ordinal: 0 }` regardless of the actual ordinal returned by candle, so a non-zero ordinal device collapses to 0 in the selection record (telemetry / receipts will be wrong).
hbr_unsatisfied: HBR-INT-002: required wire test proves ContextBundle -> ModelAdapter -> ToolGate -> ArtifactStore chain; no such evidence in MT-081 tests, and CandleRuntime does not appear to wire any ledger/ArtifactStore integration in adapter.rs (the narrative's `ledger: Arc<ProcessLedgerIntegration>` is not in the struct).
